── PASS COMPLETE · v0.2.0 · 2026-05-30 ──────────────────
Title: the project registry becomes populatable and manageable
Summary: blog.bumper can now scan a directory of git repos, interactively enroll selected ones into a central registry, and manage them from the CLI — the groundwork that lets any developer point bumper at their own projects instead of a hardcoded module list.
Project: blog.bumper
Highlights:
· git scanner discovers repos one level deep and detects .git as a file or directory, so worktrees and submodules are not silently missed
· interactive enrollment: point bumper at a projects directory, checkbox-pick the repos to track, and they land in the registry
· local-only repos (no remote) are first-class — a private devlog with no GitHub push is fully supported
· four project-* commands (add, list, info, remove) give full CLI management of enrolled projects, and never modify a project's repo on disk
Learnings:
· factoring git-detection into shared exports kept one source of truth for "what counts as a repo"
· the generic multiselect added to the wizard runner stays reusable for future pickers
· surfacing a dangling target reference in project-info is where the registry's referential-integrity gap gets caught usefully
Commit: d29229c
Tests: 172 passed · 0 failed · 0 skipped
Branch: clean
